name: PyOTP (TOTP/HOTP Python Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); pure Python OTP library

auth.notes: Library for generating/validating OTPs; no external service

performance.notes: Microsecond generation/validation; pure Python HMAC computation

agent_readiness.notes: RFC 6238 TOTP and RFC 4226 HOTP implementation. Generate and verify one-time passwords. Compatible with Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password. QR code generation for enrollment. Time-window tolerance for clock drift. Simple API: totp.now(), totp.verify(). Used for MFA implementation in web apps. Pure Python — no dependencies. The standard TOTP library for Python.

gotchas: Doesn't handle QR code rendering (use qrcode library for that)
Must securely store shared secrets (database responsibility)
Time synchronization required (server clock drift can cause failures)
best_when: Adding TOTP-based MFA to Python web applications
Generating codes compatible with Google Authenticator
Any app needing RFC 6238 compliant OTP generation
avoid_when: When a complete auth service handles MFA (Auth0, Cognito)
summary: PyOTP is the standard Python library for RFC 6238 TOTP and RFC 4226 HOTP one-time password generation and verification. Compatible with Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password. Simple totp.now()/totp.verify() API. Pure Python, no dependencies. The go-to library for adding MFA to Python web applications.
